data.description:
## Problem
The completion-verification close gate (_completion_precheck in src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py) always verifies against ref='HEAD', fetch=False (an immutable snapshot of the CURRENT worktree tree) and runs as a static file analysis that CANNOT execute git. There is no way to point the close-time verification at the ticket's OWN change (its commit, or its diff range vs a base).

## Why this is a defect (observed)
For a STACKED epic landed as a relation chain (epic bbf1: stories ef33<-ce4d<-94f9<-6673<-dfb9), each story's acceptance criteria are scoped to THAT story's contribution — several literally say 'no core module modified by this story — verified by git diff --name-only' (a whitelist AC). After all five stories merge, closing an EARLIER story fails completion verification because:
 1. the verifier sees the CUMULATIVE main tree, where a LATER story legitimately modified files outside the earlier story's whitelist (e.g. closing ce4d/S2 flagged applier.py/fetcher.py/run_differs.py/_attestation.py/apply_inbound_records.py + _backend_registry.py as 'modified outside AC4 whitelist' — but those are S3/S4's commits, not S2's); and
 2. the AC's own verification mechanism is 'git diff --name-only', which the verifier explicitly cannot run ('the AC4 verification mechanism is git diff --name-only which I cannot run').

The work is correct — each story's COMMIT touched only its scoped files (git diff --name-only <sha>^ <sha> confirms it) — but the verifier cannot see per-commit boundaries from a cumulative static tree. Today the only workaround is to hand-write an evidence comment on every affected ticket restating the per-commit git diff, which is toil and defeats the point of codebase-verifiable ACs.

## Proposed fix
Let the close specify the verification source/code-state target, e.g.:
 - rebar transition <id> closed --ref <commit>  (verify against a specific commit's tree), and/or
 - rebar transition <id> closed --diff-base <sha> (scope AC verification to the ticket's own diff range), defaulting to the ticket's rebar-ticket commit (or its merge commit) when discoverable.
And/or give the verifier git access so 'git diff --name-only'-style ACs can actually be evaluated against the ticket's commit rather than a static cumulative snapshot. This restores a legitimate close path for per-story ACs in a stacked landing without operator-attestation comments.

## Repro
1. Land a stacked epic where story N asserts 'only files X modified by this story (git diff)'.
2. After all stories merge, rebar transition <story N> in_progress closed.
3. Completion verification FAILS citing later-story files as out-of-whitelist modifications, and notes it cannot run git diff.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The close command accepts a way to target the ticket's own code state (a commit ref and/or diff base) for completion verification, documented in the close/gate docs.
- [ ] With the per-commit target, closing a stacked-epic story whose diff is correctly scoped PASSES completion verification WITHOUT a manual operator-attestation comment (regression test over a synthetic stacked-diff fixture).
- [ ] Default behavior is unchanged when no target is supplied (back-compat).
- [ ] make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
data.title: Completion-verifier close gate has no configurable source target: verifies cumulative HEAD (no git), so per-story ACs of a stacked landing false-negative
